key_themes: Analyst upgrades and price target raises (avg ~$140)
Rate hikes for $800M winter fuel cost recovery
Stable regulated growth via capex, population demand, and 5-7% earnings target
Upcoming Q1 2026 earnings (May 5) with expected EPS growth
summary: Investor sentiment for Duke Energy (DUK) is mildly positive as analysts continue raising price targets (recent moves to $143 by BMO, consensus ~$140 vs current ~$128) on the back of infrastructure spending, economic growth in its territories, and a reliable dividend yield (~3.3%). The company faces near-term regulatory and PR challenges from filings to recover $800M in elevated winter fuel/power costs via rate increases (~$7-8/month for typical customers), sparking public hearings, petitions, and vocal customer frustration over rising bills. With Q1 2026 earnings due May 5 and a history of mostly beating estimates, the utility is viewed as a stable long-term hold despite customer pushback.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/DUK/)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/energy/articles/expect-duke-energys-q1-2026-165539849.html)[[3]](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8796312/duke-energy-duk-seeks-rate-increase-to-offset-800m-in-higher-costs)
sources: 15+ news articles and analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, GuruFocus, company releases, TipRanks), 12+ recent X posts (rate hike reactions, analyst notes, investor alerts)
